title: To repeal the National Voter Registration Act of 1993.
plain_summary: This bill would get rid of a law from 1993 that makes it easier for people to register to vote. Right now, that law lets people sign up to vote when they get their driver's license or visit other government offices. If this bill passes, states would not have to offer those convenient ways to register anymore. People would still be able to vote, but they might have to work harder to sign up first.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on House Administration.

sections.title: Section 1  Repeal of National Voter Registration Act of 1993
sections.text: This bill cancels the National Voter Registration Act of nineteen ninety-three. The National Voter Registration Act was a law that made it easier for people to register to vote by allowing them to register at motor vehicle offices and other government agencies. If this bill passes, that law would no longer exist.
